meanings.definition: a list of acknowledgements of those who contributed to the creation of a film (usually run at the end of the film)

examples: he was given credit for his work

meanings.definition: money available for a client to borrow
an accounting entry acknowledging income or capital items
used in the phrase `to your credit' in order to indicate an achievement deserving praise
examples: she already had several performances to her credit

meanings.definition: arrangement for deferred payment for goods and services
recognition by a college or university that a course of studies has been successfully completed; typically measured in semester hours
a short note recognizing a source of information or of a quoted passage
examples: the student's essay failed to list several important citations
the acknowledgments are usually printed at the front of a book
the article includes mention of similar clinical cases

meanings.definition: an entry on a list of persons who contributed to a film or written work
examples: the credits were given at the end of the film

meanings.definition: an estimate, based on previous dealings, of a person's or an organization's ability to fulfill their financial commitments

examples: We credited her for saving our jobs
She was not properly credited in the program

meanings.definition: have trust in; trust in the truth or veracity of
